post.headline: Turn on two-factor authentication for your account
post.subtext: Recipients can manage core sign-in settings and enable 2FA from one place before they start handling sensitive tips.
post.image_alt_text: Social graphic built from a desktop Hush Line screenshot showing the Authentication settings page. The image shows fields for changing the username and password, a warning that changing the username can affect verification status, and a visible Enable 2FA button in the middle of the page.

social.bluesky:
Protecting a tip line starts with protecting the account behind it.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication from Settings and manage sign-in details in one place.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
Protecting a tip line starts with protecting the account behind it.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication from Settings and manage core sign-in details in one place. That matters for journalists, lawyers, and other trusted recipients who need to keep access to sensitive inboxes under control.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
Protecting a tip line starts with protecting the account behind it.

Hush Line lets recipients turn on two-factor authentication and manage sign-in details from Settings, so journalists, lawyers, and other trusted recipients can lock down inbox access.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

post.rationale: The latest screenshot release is fresh for production use, but recent archive history is already heavy on directory posts, including 2026-03-30's newsroom directory post. The two freshest PR-linked candidates would repeat that same topic family, so this authentication screen is the strongest non-directory option in the shortlist. It highlights a core documented user need, account security with optional 2FA for recipients handling sensitive disclosures, and the above-the-fold desktop view is clear, light, and non-admin.
post.copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.
post.title: Settings - Authentication (artvandelay)

summary: Use the desktop Authentication settings screen for a recipient-facing post about enabling two-factor authentication, which keeps variety after several recent directory posts.
